title: Velvety Polish Cabbage Bowl
description: A comforting bowl of silky braised cabbage with a subtle hint of garam masala, finished with smoked paprika and fresh herbs for a velvety finish.
instructions: Wash the cabbage and slice it into thin ribbons, about 1/4 inch thick.
In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and ginger, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Sprinkle in the garam masala and smoked paprika, tossing to coat the aromatics for 30 seconds.
Add the sliced cabbage to the pot, stirring to combine and coat with the spice mixture.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat and cover.
Let the cabbage steam, partially covered, for 10–12 minutes or until it has softened but still retains some bite.
Stir in the apple cider vinegar and maple syrup, and let the flavors meld for 2 minutes.
Remove the lid, increase the heat slightly, and simmer uncovered for 3–5 minutes to thicken the sauce to a velvety consistency.
Season the bowl with salt and freshly ground pepper to taste.
Transfer the cooked cabbage to bowl(s), top the center with a handful of raw, thinly sliced cabbage for a crisp contrast, and sprinkle chopped fresh dill over the top before serving.
